title: BEM-872 curator roadmap request
objective:
Create file governance/audit/mailbox/to_curator/BEM872_ROADMAP_AGREEMENT_REQUEST.md with content # BEM-872 ROADMAP AGREEMENT REQUEST
Status: SENT_TO_CURATOR
Route: External GPT auditor -> Curator -> internal contour -> Curator -> external mailbox
Objective: agree project development roadmap with internal contour under BEM-871 curator-mediated protocol.
Operator requirements:
- Use operator protocol remarks about Director/Worker contours, Curator as worker-contour hub, Director-level aggregation, Runtime Registry v2, risk lanes, and product repos.
- Use Business Model Canvas: customer segments, value proposition, channels, customer relationships, revenue streams, key resources, key activities, key partners, cost structure.
- Use world practice for multiagent systems: autonomy levels, capability matrix, deterministic-first engineering, human augmentation, orchestrated agents, interaction logging, guardrails, evaluation, and staged autonomy.
- If there are major strategic variants, list them as options with tradeoffs.
Required Curator final mailbox: governance/audit/mailbox/from_curator/BEM872_ROADMAP_AGREEMENT_RESPONSE.md
Required final status: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES or BLOCKED_WITH_REASON.
Required sections: Findings, Evidence, Roadmap, Strategic variants, Recommendation.
No issue comments.

Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM872_INTERNAL_CONTOUR_ROADMAP_RESULT.md with content # BEM-872 INTERNAL CONTOUR ROADMAP RESULT
Status: INTERNAL_RESULT_READY
Findings:
- Roadmap should keep ai-devops-system as control-plane and move products into registered product repos.
- Director contour is an upper-level processing and decision layer; Worker contour contains Curator, Analyst, Auditor, Executor roles.
- Production readiness must precede broad autonomy: lint-gate, idempotency, checkpoint/resume, registry, role contracts, mailbox protocol.
- Business Model Canvas should be used not as a separate business-plan document, but as a governance canvas attached to product and client pilots.
- Agentic implementation should be staged: deterministic/rule/constraint logic first, LLM only where natural-language reasoning is needed.
Evidence:
- governance/protocols/CURATOR_MEDIATED_INTERNAL_AUDIT_PROTOCOL.md
- uploaded operator protocol: protokol_razvitiya_multiagentnoy_sistemy-1.docx
- uploaded AI agents summary: РЕЗЮМЕ.docx
- external Business Model Canvas article supplied by operator
Roadmap:
- P0 stabilize runtime and proof contour.
- P1 formalize roles, registry, board/director layer.
- P2 product repo registry and first business-domain pilot.
- P3 multi-client SLA, external storage only if file registry becomes limiting.
Strategic variants:
- Variant A: control-plane first. Safest, slower business value.
- Variant B: pilot-first. Faster business value, higher architecture debt risk.
- Variant C: immediate multiagent autonomy. Not recommended before controls.
Recommendation:
- Use hybrid A+B: finish P0 controls, then run one vertical pilot through Board/Director/Worker contours with BMC and strict curator-mediated mailbox.
No issue comments.
